lifecycle_layer_legend.L0: computed state (trigger nodes; deterministic functions of Q1-Q3)
lifecycle_layer_legend.L1: trigger validation (BLOCK cells only; not used for nodes — see cross_method_rules.json)
lifecycle_layer_legend.L2: configuration assertion (HC/MC nodes; standard runtime layer)

extraction_notes: v1 §4.1 aggregate stats (DEFAULT=102, DERIVED=74) do not match row-by-row count (DEFAULT=116, DERIVED=70). v1 row-level data taken as ground truth; aggregate stats imprecise. Per-method actual: LCA 29/30, LCC 28/33, S-LCA 59/7 (incl. 1 Trigger each).
Field gaps (initial commit): 142/186 nodes have field=null + field_status='not_specified_in_v2_section_8'. To be resolved in dedicated turn (file: field_gaps.md). NO field names invented.

v2 §8 fields not assigned to any Phase 1 node (initial commit): lca.system_boundary_physical, lca.capital_goods.amortization_period, lcc.capital_goods.amortization_period — see review feedback in commit `bugfix-1`.
Trigger nodes (lca_t1, lcc_trig_01, slca_t_01) marked lifecycle_layer='L0' per design choice 5. Schema enum extended to {L0, L1, L2, L3} from v2 §9.1 baseline {L1, L2, L3}.

ID convention normalized to lowercase snake_case with method prefix (lca_/lcc_/slca_), per v2 §9.1 example. v1 used 'HC-XX' colliding across methods; v2 §4 source_nodes also normalized in cross_method_rules.json (commit 1, second file).
source_section: v1 mostly omits the deliverable prefix (e.g., '§3.2'); prepended D4.1/D4.2/D4.3 by method per design choice 3.
Default values restructured to dict for discriminative DERIVED nodes (matches v2 §9.1 lca_mc_02 example); preserved as string for DEFAULT and for DERIVED with single-condition gating.
Per-flow flag (per_flow=true) on 11 nodes per v1 §5.4 list. These are evaluated per case.flows[*] not at case level.

Eight nodes carry per-node extraction_notes flagging non-trivial extraction choices (v1 internal inconsistencies preserved, simultaneous-activation pairs, etc.). One additional node (slca_hc_10) reclassified after design review found v1 trigger=Q7 inconsistent with D4.3 §4.1 verbatim; corrected to trigger=Q3.SOC.

POST-REVIEW BUGFIX: Three nodes updated per field_classification.md review (slca_hc_01, lca_hc_18, lcc_hc_07). slca_hc_01 now sets field='slca.logic' default='comparative' (closes IR-03). lca_hc_18 and lcc_hc_07 each get an additional_fields entry for capital_goods.amortization_period (default 20 years, aligned for IR-13/B-06).

POST-REVIEW SCHEMA EXTENSION: optional 'additional_fields' list added to node schema for nodes that legitimately set multiple sub-fields of the same conceptual area (e.g., capital_goods.{included, amortization_period}). Preserves the 'one primary field per node' convention and keeps the 186 count unchanged. The validation script collects primary + additional_fields when building the node_fields set.

nodes.default_value: Multi-FU analysis allowed when actors have distinct outputs
Reference flow tied to Functional Unit
Avoided products documented when system expansion used
Substitution data quality and source documented
Monte Carlo mandatory if Q4 in {C,D,E} OR Q6b<TRL9

default_value.q6b in {TRL7-8, TRL5-6, TRL<5}: Capital goods full inclusion + scale-up frameworks

additional_fields.notes: Per D4.1 §5.5; aligned with lcc.capital_goods.amortization_period via IR-13.

nodes.default_value: Frontier categories (microplastic, soil, biodiversity) per sector overlay
nodes.extraction_notes: Sector-specific overlay; full Q6a-driven logic deferred to sector_overlays.json (v2 §7)

nodes.default_value: Break-even distance sensitivity if Q7 in {B,C,D}

nodes.extraction_notes: Mirrors lca_t1; v1 lists both T1 and MC-01 covering ILCD situation; preserved as separate per v1

nodes.default_value: LCA-LCC integration matrix-based UM³-LCE³-ISN if Q3.ECO+Q3.ENV; standalone otherwise
Background database: Ecoinvent 3.10 cut-off + system expansion
Function-oriented FU implementation (single option per D4.1 for IS)
Process-LCA preferred for foreground; IO for screening
Sensitivity on assumptions, mandatory frontier categories

nodes.extraction_notes: v1 §5.2 + v2 §4.2 CIR-05: MC-13 and MC-14 BOTH activated by Q4=D (not alternatives — coexist per D4.1 §5.3.2 vs §5.3.3)
v1 §5.2 + v2 §4.2 CIR-05: MC-14 paired with MC-13 (both PEF CFF on Q4=D)

nodes.default_value: Uncertainty: OAT first + Morris GSA on critical parameters
MFA backbone activated when multi-actor (Q1 in {B,C,D,E})
nodes.extraction_notes: v1 marks DEFAULT but description has Q1 conditional; preserved as DEFAULT (universal mandate, trivially satisfied for Q1=A)

nodes.default_value: Scale-up frameworks (Six-Tenths, Lang, CEPCI) active if Q6b<=TRL7
Background futurisation SSP/RCP if Q2=D OR asset lifetime>15y
Modular life-cycle structure (raw, proc, distrib, use, EoL)

nodes.extraction_notes: Field renamed from lca.reference_scenario to lca.reference_scenario.type per Mirko design call (field_gaps round, MODIFICA 2): paired with lca_mc_27 setting lca.reference_scenario.content. Avoids dotted-path duplication anti-pattern.

nodes.default_value: Geographic differentiation in LCIA when relevant
Spatial coupling: Q7 in {C,D} -> GIS-coupled mandatory
AWARE water stress mandatory if Q6a=Wastewater/biofactories
MFCA bridge ISO 14051 active if Q3.ECO=true
UM³-LCE³-ISN matrix integration when Q3.ECO + Q3.ENV both
nodes.extraction_notes: v1 marks DEFAULT but description has Q3 conditional; preserved as DEFAULT per v1

nodes.default_value: Hybrid technique selection (default Integrated for IS multi-actor)
NDAs in place before confidential data exchange
Break-even distance sensitivity if Q7 in {B,C,D}

additional_fields.notes: Per D4.2 §4.2; aligned with lca.capital_goods.amortization_period via IR-13/B-06.

nodes.default_value: Market price = upstream proxy (no double counting)
Allocation rule linked to LCC type in Charter
Reference scenario for avoided cost documented
Avoidable/Unavoidable test for each secondary flow (Q5 != e)
TRL<7 -> Six-Tenths, Lang Factors, CEPCI mandatory
Mandatory harmonization protocol (currency, ref year, CBS)
Full KPI suite at all 3 levels (NPV, IRR/MIRR, DPP, LCOE/LCOP)
Partner-specific discount rates at entity level
Perfect systemic LCA-LCC alignment when Q3.ENV+Q3.ECO both
Eco-efficiency uses annualized costs not NPV
Transfer price as central sensitivity if Q5=c
Zero-cost flows explicitly identified + sensitivity (mandatory if Q5 in {a,b})
Modified production -> multifunctional remodel if Q5=d
Ex-ante: trigger conditions for iterative update if Q2 in {B,C,D}

nodes.trigger_condition: (flow.q5 == 'b') OR (case.q3.eco == true AND case.q3.env == false AND case.q3.soc == false)

nodes.extraction_notes: Mirrors lcc_trig_01; v1 lists both as the LCC-type derivation

nodes.default_value: CBS: IS-Expanded default for IS context

nodes.extraction_notes: v1 default text refers to 'Avoidable/Unavoidable per Q5 answer' — restated per Q5 option for clarity

nodes.default_value: Distribution defaults: Triangular for sensitivities, Log-normal for prices, Normal for engineering
LCA-LCC integration mode: Q3.ENV+ECO -> matrix-based UM³; otherwise Separate
Quality correction: physical + market price differentials

nodes.extraction_notes: Sets slca.logic='comparative' (universal IS S-LCA mandate, closes IR-03). v1 default_value text was 'Comparative assessment logic (relative not absolute)' — kept as documentation in the diagnosis, replaced by formal value 'comparative' for engine consumption.

nodes.default_value: Both scenarios shared FU + boundaries + temporal/geographical

nodes.trigger_condition: case.q3.soc == true AND (case.q3.env == true OR case.q3.eco == true)
nodes.default_value: FU from LCA/LCC retained as common reference

nodes.trigger_condition: case.q3.soc == true AND (case.q3.env == true OR case.q3.eco == true)

nodes.default_value: System boundaries explicitly incorporate both organizational and territorial dimensions (universal S-LCA mandate)
nodes.extraction_notes: v1 marked trigger as Q7 (geographic spread); D4.3 §4.1 para 3 verbatim ('System boundaries explicitly incorporate both organizational and territorial dimensions') is a universal 'shall' when S-LCA is active. Reclassified to DERIVED-on-q3 per design review. Q7 affects HOW territorial scope is operationalized (single-site vs cross-border) but NOT whether it's included — that's a separate concern handled by IR-12 (Transport-Spatial Co-Modeling, L2 cross-method rule).

nodes.default_value: Multi-role actors: no overlooking, no double-counting
S-LCI: combination of primary and secondary data
Each data point linked to indicator and stakeholder group
Conservative under uncertainty (no positive without evidence)
Indicator results preserve link to stakeholder/data
Trade-offs between stakeholders identified and analysed
No aggregation into single metric without consideration
Distribution of impacts as key aspect
Identify social hotspots and improvement opportunities

nodes.trigger_condition: case.q3.env == true AND case.q3.eco == true AND case.q3.soc == true
nodes.default_value: LCSA: shared FU + consistent boundaries + scenario-based
Results framed as comparative outcomes, NOT absolute
Comparative orientation (mandatory for IS, default for all)
Engagement level: analytical default; consulted/participatory advanced
Indicator types: quantitative + semi-quantitative + qualitative + mixed
Proxy use rule (when direct measurements not available)
Uncertainty: qualitative context-sensitive (S-LCA-specific, NOT Monte Carlo)
